name: Calvin Jackson
abstract: John Calvin Jackson (May 26, 1919 – December 9, 1985) was an American jazz pianist, composer, and bandleader.

works.t: Faust: Act IV. Vous qui faites l'enformie

concerts.label: Paul Draper, Tap Dancer, and Larry Adler, Harmonica

works.t: 8 Piano Pieces, Op. 76: 6. Intermezzo in A Major
Mignon: Act II. Me voici dans son boudoir (Gavotte)

concerts.label: Dance Festival to Benefit Russian War Relief
Carnegie Pop Concert: Salute to Fats Waller

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Appleyard
about_quotes.evidence: I got a job with a man called Calvin Jackson... he hired me to be a guest on the television series. He liked me so much he asked me to join the quartet, which I did, and I went with him for about a year or so
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Appleyard interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 4, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER mentions Calvin Jackson in the context of Duke Ellington's history; Interviewee does not claim a personal or professional relationship with Calvin Jackson, only discussing Duke Ellington's arrangements.

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: Calvin Jackson was the only black musician working the studios, and he was working at MGM as a composer and arranger... And after Calvin -- Calvin was out there during the time I was freelancing

about_quotes.speaker: Earl Watkins
about_quotes.evidence: The second week they had Calvin Jackson, the pianist. He had written for the studios and he was a fine arranger in Hollywood and then he went back up to Canada. He is a fine pianist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Earl Watkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Joseph "Fan" Bourgeau
about_quotes.evidence: And Calvin Jackson and he was a piano player. Ed Moishier. I'm talking about the way back you know. And all them was fellas that hanged around the district

about_quotes.speaker: Dorothy Donegan
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER mentions Calvin Jackson in a list of pianists; INTERVIEWEE responds generally about getting paid extra, not describing a personal relationship.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dorothy Donegan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
